setting.note: Avonlea is invented, but Montgomery built it from Cavendish on Prince Edward Island, where the farmhouse she had in mind is now the preserved Green Gables Heritage Place, the most visited place on the island. The bbox runs from the north-shore Cavendish landscape south to Charlottetown, the real capital where Anne sits her Queen's exams. The first book here set in Canada.

rights.originalWork: Public domain. L. M. Montgomery, first published 1908; the author died in 1942.
rights.territoryNotes: Public domain in the United States, and in the United Kingdom and the European Union, where the author died more than 70 years ago (1942).
rights.attribution: L. M. Montgomery, Anne of Green Gables (1908). Text from Project Gutenberg.

palette.accentSourceQuote: Very green and neat and precise was that yard
palette.note: The green of the gables and the Cuthbert yard, taken straight from the text. Clears WCAG AA on paper.

anne.name: Anne Shirley
anne.colorSourceQuote: of very thick, decidedly red hair

matthew.name: Matthew Cuthbert
matthew.colorSourceQuote: long iron-gray hair that touched his stooping shoulders
